##NAMES=Segerstromite
##RRUFFID=R130753
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_3_(As^5+^O_4_)_2_[As^3+^(OH)_3_]_2_
##LOCALITY=Cobriza mine, Sacramento District, Copiapó Province, Chile
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Bob Jenkins
##DESCRIPTION=Colorless tetrahedral crystals, associated with talmessite, vladimirite, and apatite. Type sample
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R130753
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=Ca_2.98_(AsO_4_)_2.00_[As(OH)_3_]_2.00_ ; The darker phase is fluorapatite.
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 10.7627 b: 10.7627 c: 10.7627 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 1246.71 crystal system: cubic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
